name: Christina Reynolds, EMILY's List
subject: A whole new level of voter suppression
disclaimer_text: Paid for by EMILY's List [www
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c50ab/24c69fd9/106459e87/189650d8/3907997245/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEO10N_digital_email_20180305_WISpecial_inact&source=WQEFEO10N_digital_email_20180305_WISpecial_inact")
---

Peter,

Wisconsin Gov. Scott Walker is trying to subvert democracy.

He's refusing to hold special elections to fill two state legislative seats that have been vacant since December, when the previous holders resigned to take jobs in Walker's administration.

Why wouldn't Walker call the elections to fill the seats? Maybe because in January our candidate Patty Schachtner flipped a Wisconsin state Senate seat from red to blue in a 27-point swing from Trump's win there in 2016.

We need to send Walker, and any other Republican thinking of copying him, a message that they can't stop the wave of pro-choice Democratic women we are recruiting to defeat them.

**[Donate $3 to help elect our women to state and local office and build the blue wave Republicans are so afraid of.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c50ab/24c69fd9/106459e87/189650d9/3907997245/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180305_email_WISpecial?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+elect+our+women+to+stat&utm_campaign=WQEFEO10N_digital_email_20180305_WISpecial_inact&source=WQEFEO10N_digital_email_20180305_WISpecial_inact")**

Walker's so scared of our electoral power that he'd rather leave constituents in those districts without representation for _a whole year_ than hold an election now.

He called Patty's win a "wake-up call."

Apparently it wasn't a wake-up call to give up on his anti-woman policies.

Instead, he's taking his voter suppression tactics to a whole new level -- by not even allowing a vote to happen!

Enough. Time to show Walker and his fellow Republicans that we're done with their attacks on women and our democracy.

**Donate $3 to help elect pro-choice Democratic women to state and local office and replace even more Republicans:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Replace-Even-More-Republicans](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c50ab/24c69fd9/106459e87/189650da/3907997245/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180305_email_WISpecial?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgReplaceEvenMoreR&utm_campaign=WQEFEO10N_digital_email_20180305_WISpecial_inact&source=WQEFEO10N_digital_email_20180305_WISpecial_inact")**

Thanks,

Christina Reynolds
Vice President of Communications, EMILY's List

name: Jeanne Shaheen
subject: Putin can't bully me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shaheen for Senate
body:
| | |
---
Friends: I'm on Vladimir Putin's blacklist - and that's just fine with me.

When our U.S. ambassador to Russia invited me to travel to Moscow last December, I learned my visa was rejected, and I was told that I was banned from stepping foot on Russian soil.

**Why? Because I stood up to Putin. Because I demanded that Russia be penalized for meddling in the 2016 elections. Because I did what Donald Trump refused to do.
**
Friends, Putin should know one thing: No bullying by the Kremlin will _ever_ stop me from holding Russia accountable for interfering with our democracy. And no threats will _ever_ keep me from speaking out against coordinated, political, flat-out dangerous attacks on special counsel Bob Mueller's investigation.
**
Our democracy is under assault - so you better believe that I'll never be silenced, but I need to know if you're with me. Tell me:**
| | **Should we end Russian interference in our elections?**

| | | [YES](https://surveys.signforgood.com/cjs-russian-interference?code=jtk837-su-nd&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500)

name: The White House
subject: ‘This will be remembered’
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=77b2883089df76bc179465a507554f169a21cd672d8349156f3e2c7c4718777eacfae49e55caf62fe1e70fbdeee0f9dd3248c8e802c93a20

‘This will be remembered’

President Donald J. Trump hosted Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for a bilateral visit at the White House yesterday. The two leaders discussed the imminent move of the U.S. Embassy in Israel to its capital city of Jerusalem, as well as the path to peace for the Middle East.

“I want to tell you that the Jewish people have a long memory,” Prime Minister Netanyahu said. “We remember 70 years ago, President Harry S. Truman was the first leader to recognize the Jewish state. And we remember how a few weeks ago, President Donald J. Trump recognized Jerusalem as Israel’s capital,” he continued.

“Mr. President, this will be remembered by our people throughout the ages. And as you just said, others talked about it. You did it.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=77b2883089df76bc0bc375b1968390c35b8983aad2af13f1c1a8e730e9777ecc9e9cf193de1aeec6d7533b8cf4aa5739cd12197233064543
The greatest challenge to the Middle East is encapsulated in one word, says Prime Minister Netanyahu.

‘Needed attention’ on infrastructure

Two top officials in Oklahoma—the state’s Governor, Mary Fallin, along with Secretary of Transportation Mike Patterson—write that “infrastructure improvement is a priority issue because of its direct impact on economic growth and stability as well as the safety and well-being of our citizens.”

The two state leaders make a convincing case:

- In 2011, 706 of Oklahoma’s 6,800 highway bridges were structurally deficient. That number is coming down to 187 under Gov. Fallin, and the President’s “plan will allow Oklahoma to continue to prioritize infrastructure projects.”
- President Trump’s proposal includes traditional transportation such as roads and transit, but it also supports broadband internet, water and power systems, and other indispensable pieces for a modern infrastructure system.
- The Administration’s plan will benefit states such as Oklahoma by directing 25 percent of Federal funding—about $50 billion—for rural projects.

The bottom line? “We support the Trump administration’s infrastructure proposal for starting the dialogue about the need for these improvements,” the pair write.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=77b2883089df76bc65d0aadab0bfeb2866801cfa8cb96330ee8c67e7cd1c91f9d8d67db1904c572da9a27914b12a6b7dba9675630d2c765a
Learn more about why Oklahoma’s leaders are with President Trump on infrastructure.

America to overtake Russia on oil

The United States will eclipse Russia to become the largest oil producer in the world by 2023, The Wall Street Journal reported yesterday.

This latest headline is yet more evidence that President Trump’s plan to achieve American energy dominance is paying off just 13 months into his term. “Together, we are going to start a new energy revolution—one that celebrates American production on American soil,” the President said last year.

According to the Journal, “U.S. crude production is expected to reach a record of 12.1 million barrels a day in 2023, up about 2 million barrels a day from this year.” What will that mean for America? “Once heavily dependent on imports from the Middle East, the U.S. is getting closer to achieving its goal of producing enough crude to meet domestic demand for refined products like gasoline,” the Journal says.

Learn more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=77b2883089df76bce16affc045924b2a267d2782a5d3e7f94dd8ef1c5298bc49854c1a7d4b443965fdf6b82a19ddff92362fe8a051be4a8d
The U.S. will be the world’s largest oil producer by 2023, says IEA

Photo of the Day

Vice President Mike Pence at AIPAC 2018 | March 5, 2018 (Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen)

POTUS TODAY

This afternoon, President Trump will participate in an expanded bilateral meeting with Prime Minister Stefan Löfven of Sweden. Later, the President will meet with Swedish business representatives before holding a joint press conference with Prime Minister Löfven.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=77b2883089df76bc97909ba170c7e833c393ea1029458322a2c0469878f7f3b13fbd9bdc766cab7f5a2efb87871f3ca7458d195772daa151
Watch live at 3:30pm ET.

This morning, the Vice President participated in a phone call with President Petro Poroshenko of Ukraine. The Vice President then travels to Omaha, Nebraska, to deliver keynote remarks at an America First Policies “Tax Cuts to Put America First” event.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=77b2883089df76bc9984de590afbf6606bc3314132db19f25ef9d4b6f009a54a9acefaa26cf1ed2d697e1a09c23fc1075172df4274112a10
Privacy Policy |

Contact the White House |

name: Ben Jealous
subject: The start of something big
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friends,
Today, Texas voters will head to the polls to vote in the 2018 midterm primary. This year, an unprecedented number of Democrats have their sights set on taking seats that have remained red for years.
Even though the last Democratic presidential candidate to win the state was Jimmy Carter in 1976, there is a resistance growing across the nation, and it is clear that politicians who refuse to stand up to Donald Trump face an energized electorate.
Last year, I was awestruck by results across the country, demonstrating that voters are fed up with the extremists that took power in 2016. I genuinely believe this is the start of something big.
**[I'm fired up. If you are too, will you chip in $5 to my campaign so we can send a message to Larry Hogan that his refusal to stand up to Trump will cost him his job?](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a74d/7ab0e2c4/7f5fc9d3/349a0b78/3581608223/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st3-18?refcode=OHQ03068D")**
Alabama voters rejected Roy Moore and his contemptible past, Ralph Northam swept Virginia, and Danica Roem became the country's first openly transgender state legislator.
**If that 's not a blue tide rising, I don't know what is.**
But we can't rest now. We need your help to turn Maryland blue again. Maryland families have had enough of Republican policies that hurt the economy, the environment, our young people, and our families. And we've had enough of politicians who refuse to stand up to the likes of Donald Trump and his harmful policies.
**[There is a lot more work to do before this year's election, but I am confident with you on my side, we can take back Maryland. We can and will build a tide that will raise all boats. Chip in to my campaign today.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a74d/7ab0e2c4/7f5fc9d3/349a0b78/3581608223/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st3-18?refcode=OHQ03068D")**
Thank you,
Ben Jealous

Clean energy is the future of New Mexico's economy, but President Trump continues to rollback programs that can help this growing industry flourish.

New Mexico legislators are working to reverse the tide and reinstate tax credits for solar panels, which could reinvigorate New Mexico's economy and protect our environment.

[**We need to ensure Governor Martinez signs this bipartisan bill and supports clean energy. Will you sign my petition today and urge Governor Martinez to say YES to solar energy?**](http://nm.martinheinrich. Martin

name: Bob Poe, Andrew Gillum for Governor
subject: The candidate we've all been waiting for...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb316c/9e00690/42cdf1b7/2403ae8a/1250404804/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180306_Poe_fnd_gq_act__BP&source=em18_180306_Poe_fnd_gq_act__BP")
Hello,

I wasn't planning on endorsing any of the candidates in this primary. I know them all personally and like them as people. But with what's going on, I needed to come off the sidelines.

We have a unique and historic opportunity to elect a bold candidate that strongly believes in our Democratic values, and isn't afraid to share them from the brightest blue cities to the deepest red counties of our state -- and everywhere in between.

**[That's why today I'm proud to endorse Andrew Gillum for Governor.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb316c/9e00690/42cdf1b7/2403ae8d/1250404804/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/andrew-gillum-bob-poe?utm_campaign=em18_180306_Poe_fnd_gq_act__BP&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em18_180306_Poe_fnd_gq_act__BP&utm_content=2+-+Thatrsquos+why+today+Irsquom+proud+to+en")**[ ](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb316c/9e00690/42cdf1b7/2403ae8c/1250404804/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/andrew-gillum-bob-poe?utm_campaign=em18_180306_Poe_fnd_gq_act__BP&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em18_180306_Poe_fnd_gq_act__BP&utm_content=3+-++")

[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb316c/9e00690/42cdf1b7/2403ae8f/1250404804/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/andrew-gillum-bob-poe?utm_campaign=em18_180306_Poe_fnd_gq_act__BP&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em18_180306_Poe_fnd_gq_act__BP&utm_content=4")

I've been on the front lines with you as a volunteer and activist. Behind the scenes, I've been a fundraiser and donor to many Democratic candidates and causes. I even had the honor to serve as your state party Chair. And as a candidate for Congress, I proposed a ban on assault weapons after the tragedy at Pulse.

And, in all those years I've never seen a candidate quite like Andrew.

As a student leader at Florida A&M, he took it to Jeb Bush and the Republicans when they removed the state's Affirmative Action guidelines.

As the Mayor of Tallahassee, he took the NRA to court and won.

In the last several weeks, he's taken it to the streets arm-in-arm with the students at Marjory Stoneman Douglas, both in Tallahassee and in Broward.

Andrew is the candidate we've been waiting for.

I always say, "if you want to make a difference, you've got to be the difference."

It was time for me, and now it's time for you, to be the difference for Andrew.

subject: Our opponent is blaming abortions for school shootings (seriously)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
**Get this:** [On Friday,](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=NJNFVbGRHycTYCPCieiPY9M%2FylWFANEX) one of the top GOP candidates trying to defeat Tim in November stood on the floor of the Virginia House of Delegates and actually said that _abortions are to blame for mass shootings._

**And that 's not all:** He actually suggested that Planned Parenthood - which provides lifesaving health care to more than 4.5 million people worldwide every year - is equivalent to the NRA.

The so-called "moderate" candidate in the Republican primary said that abortion, divorce and welfare are what leads to gun violence and mass shootings.

Not guns.

But you know what's really scary? The fact that the right-wing echo chamber made sure the video went viral - [it has racked up more than 12 million views on Facebook.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=fPSV4ine4EboAVBTvCDAONM%2FylWFANEX)

This is what we're up against: an out-of-control far right hellbent on pushing the NRA's radical agenda and stopping any meaningful action to reduce gun violence.

We're facing GOP opponents who vote to block universal background checks, who host "shoot and greet" fundraisers, and who respond to national tragedies by dialing up divisive, loud talking points hand-delivered by the gun lobby.

[And 12,000,000 people are hearing them.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=wcNNWzlo9GLVHXGebCoLzNM%2FylWFANEX)

When our opponents have that kind of megaphone, we have to step up to get our message out.

name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: Will you help elect a record number of women this year, Peter?

body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c508a/24c69fd9/1055e1e46/18964321/3523457830/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQENEO05N_digital_email_20180306_WHMkickoffSO_inact&source=WQENEO05N_digital_email_20180306_WHMkickoffSO_inact")
---

Peter,

It's Women's History Month!

As we honor all that women have achieved and consider how far we have to go, I want to highlight a historic moment that means a lot to this community: **Anita Hill and the 1992 elections.**

Anita became a household name in 1991 when she testified about then-Supreme Court nominee Clarence Thomas sexually harassing her at work.

The all-white, all-male Senate panel treated her despicably. She was called a "scorned woman" and "erotomaniac" for telling the truth about what happened to her. Other women with similar stories about Thomas weren't called to testify at all.

Though not all women across the country stood by Anita, many did and demanded change. Too many of us knew how it felt to experience sexual harassment or be silenced by powerful men but never felt we could say anything.

Seven such women were Democratic representatives in the U.S. House -- and they marched over to their Senate colleagues to demand Anita be heard and taken seriously.

Unfortunately, Clarence Thomas still joined the Supreme Court, where he's been a reliably anti-woman, conservative vote ever since.

But the following year, in 1992, women took their disappointment and outrage and channeled it into electing four new EMILY's List women to the Senate and 20 to the House.

**The parallels between 1992 and 2018 are unmistakable.**

We are in a moment where women across the country have channeled their disappointment over Donald Trump's election and their outrage at the epidemic of sexual harassment and assault into political action -- and more and more women and good men are standing with those voices for change.

This year, our work as a community is to turn that energy into wins at the ballot box.

**Add your name to say you're ready to help make 2018 a sea change for every woman and man by electing a record number of pro-choice Democratic women:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Make-2018-Sea-Change](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c508a/24c69fd9/1055e1e46/18964322/3523457830/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20180306_email_WHMkickoff?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgMake2018SeaChang&utm_campaign=WQENEO05N_digital_email_20180306_WHMkickoffSO_inact&source=WQENEO05N_digital_email_20180306_WHMkickoffSO_inact")**

Happy Women's History Month!

Thanks for all you do,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: another failure of leadership
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __
Instead of acting to prevent more gun violence tragedies, this week Senate Republican leader Mitch McConnell has cleared the Senate schedule to debate and vote on a bill that rolls back many of the common sense bank regulations passed after the 2008 financial meltdown and weakens oversight of foreign banks operating in the U.S.

At a time when Americans are still reeling from the Florida shooting tragedy, it is shocking that the Senate has turned instead to legislation that the Congressional Budget Office says will increase the chances of another taxpayer bailout of the banking industry.

I would say that this is difficult to explain, but the truth is that this is entirely consistent with what we have seen from this Republican-controlled Congress. The Republican leadership has focused at every opportunity on the agenda of special interests, not the concerns of the majority of Americans.

[**Add your name here if you agree that the decision to reward Wall Street and the NRA by bringing up special interest bills, instead of common sense guns safety laws supported by an overwhelming majority of Americans, is plain wrong.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de9187b/70cdc2ba/460215d6/3903a38/2685603268/VEsH/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/tell-congress---act-on-gun-safety-not-banking?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Add+your+name+here+if+you+agree+that+the&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180306&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180306")

But students here in Maryland and around the country are actively working to make sure the voices of the American people cannot be denied. Their efforts to increase pressure for long-overdue common sense action on gun violence are important. And we need to support them.

This is the most serious student movement that I have seen during my time in public office, and if sustained, it will be a powerful force for real change.

There must be a political price to pay for ignoring the pleas of the majority of Americans by refusing to take real action against gun violence. The NRA's grip on Congress must be broken. NRA leader Wayne LaPierre's outrageous attacks against students crossed the line of common decency, and revealed what we already knew -- that he is entirely out of touch with the American people.

[**Our goal must be to continue to work together to show that people power will ultimately triumph over the special interests that dominate the GOP-controlled Congress today. Sign here to send a message to Republican leadership that we won 't stop until we succeed.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de9187b/70cdc2ba/460215d6/3903a39/2685603268/VEsE/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/tell-congress---act-on-gun-safety-not-banking?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Our+goal+must+be+to+continue+to+work+tog&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180306&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180306")

Thank you for all you do.

Chris Van Hollen
U.S. Senator

---

---

10605 Concord Street, Suite 202
Kensington, MD 20895
